details: The Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled & Senior Citizens launched the "Sanction of Laptops" scheme to distribute free laptops to visually challenged, hearing impairments, speech impairments, and orthopedic challenges students pursuing professional courses. The A.P. Differently Abled & Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation proposes to procure 200 laptops at ₹30,000/- each.
benefits: Category I: Parent/guardian income up to ₹15,000/- per month - Full cost covered.
Category II: Parent/guardian income from ₹15,000/- to ₹20,000/- per month - Half of the cost covered.
Category III: Parent/guardian income above ₹20,000/- per month - Full cost to be paid by the beneficiary.
eligibility: The student should be a native of Andhra Pradesh.
The student should be visually challenged, hearing challenged, speech impaired, and orthopedically challenged.
The student should be pursuing postgraduate or professional courses.
The laptop is sanctioned once in the lifetime of the student.

online: The interested student needs to visit the
Click on the "Online Applications" on the home page.
Select the category "click here to apply for differently abled schemes"
Now click on "Apply Online" respect to the scheme name.
If applicant was not register earlier
Answer the above registration question as "No".
Fill in your Aadhaar card name, Aadhaar number, Mobile number, email ID.
Create a new password for this application.
Click on "Register" After that get the registration number.
Again, ask the same "Are you registered already: Yes/No Select "Yes".
Click on "Login" and select the category again "click here to apply for differently abled schemes" .
Fill out the application form for personal employment and disability detail.

documents_required: Bonafide Certificate (issued from the recognized School/College).

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the laptop sanction scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide laptops to visually, hearing, speech, and orthopedically challenged students pursuing professional courses to enhance their educational experience.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the laptop sanction scheme?
faqs.answer: Visually challenged, hearing challenged, speech impaired, and orthopedically challenged students pursuing postgraduate or professional courses.

faqs.question: Is the laptop provided under this scheme a one-time benefit?
faqs.answer: Yes, each eligible student can receive a laptop once in their lifetime.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for a laptop?
faqs.answer: Bonafide certificate from the current institution, Income certificate of the parents/guardians, Disability certificate (Saderam certificate), Aadhar card, Contact information (cell number).

faqs.question: How will the laptops be distributed to the beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Laptops will be distributed through the Assistant Directors or District Managers of the Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled & Senior Citizens.

faqs.question: How is the scheme monitored to prevent double or false claims?
faqs.answer: Detailed records of beneficiaries are maintained by the Assistant Directors and District Managers to ensure transparency and avoid duplicate claims.

faqs.question: Can I apply for a laptop if I am studying a non-professional course?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is specifically for students pursuing postgraduate or professional courses.

faqs.question: What is the cost of the laptop provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The cost of each laptop is ₹30,000.

faqs.question: Can I choose the laptop model or brand under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the laptops are procured by APTS (Andhra Pradesh Technology Services) and distributed as per the scheme’s guidelines.

faqs.question: How long does the application process take?
faqs.answer: The duration may vary; it is advisable to contact the respective office for specific timelines.

faqs.question: Who can I contact for more information about the laptop sanction scheme?
faqs.answer: You can contact the Assistant Directors or District Managers of the A.P. Differently Abled & Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation for more details.
